paragraph: The single-family market in Paramus is where the volume sits, with 86 sales over six months at a median of $999,999. Homes are selling right at list price with a sale-to-list ratio of 1.0, and about 31 percent of them close above asking. The median time on market is 28 days, which puts this in competitive but balanced territory. You'll see a wide range across town, from $625,000 to $2,650,000, so neighborhood and condition matter significantly to where your home lands. Condos and townhouses are sparse here with only 7 sales at a median of $1,120,000, sitting at list price with no premium and a 37-day median on market. Co-ops round out the market at the entry point with 4 sales at a median of $391,250, moving more slowly at 51 days on market and with some financing constraints that buyers should understand going in. If you're comparing tiers, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family, the low-maintenance lifestyle of a condo, or the lower price point of a co-op.
